jokes.text: If we're so rich...why we can't afford no ceiling?
jokes.setup: Family discussing their wealth and financial security
jokes.punchline: Will's street-smart observation about mansion's missing ceiling
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - only Will would notice this practical detail amid wealth discussion. Great economy and specificity.

jokes.text: Really? Even with all that in common?
jokes.setup: Phil describes Lisa's father as obstinate, dictatorial old tightwad
jokes.punchline: Will points out Phil has same qualities
jokes.explanation: Sharp callback insult that recontextualizes Phil's complaint. Excellent economy and timing.

jokes.text: Really? What did he do? - He died.
jokes.setup: Will asks what improved Phil's relationship with father-in-law
jokes.punchline: Phil's matter-of-fact delivery of dark punchline
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy timing with Phil's deadpan delivery. Excellent structure and economy.

jokes.text: What you talking about? You hate liver. - Exactly.
jokes.setup: Phil compares Will's charm to liver as acquired taste
jokes.punchline: Phil reveals he hates liver, completing the insult
jokes.explanation: Well-constructed setup/payoff with Phil's characteristic dry wit completing the burn.

jokes.text: Listen, I've tried, but he likes that shirt.
jokes.setup: Hilary says they need to do something about Dad
jokes.punchline: Carlton misunderstands, thinks it's about Phil's fashion
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that fits Carlton's literal-minded character perfectly.

jokes.text: Then why did they keep trying?
jokes.setup: Hilary claims oldest is always favorite because parents got it right first time
jokes.punchline: Carlton points out logical flaw in her reasoning
jokes.explanation: Sharp logical comeback that deflates Hilary's reasoning with simple question.

jokes.text: Don't know, I'm starting to think you were adopted.
jokes.setup: Discussion about all being Phil's children equally
jokes.punchline: Carlton's cutting remark to Ashley
jokes.explanation: Standard sibling insult that fits Carlton's competitive nature.

jokes.text: Sir, that's a finger bowl.
jokes.setup: Will recommending the 'lemon soup' to impress Lisa's father
jokes.punchline: Waiter correcting Will's embarrassing mistake
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Will out of his element in fancy restaurant.

jokes.text: For 24 bucks, I want a 20-dollar bill on it.
jokes.setup: Will listing expensive pizza toppings
jokes.punchline: Fred's practical response about overpriced food
jokes.explanation: Great working-class perspective joke with perfect economy and character voice.

jokes.text: No, I'm not. I fix cars.
jokes.setup: Will calls him an automotive technician
jokes.punchline: Fred's blunt rejection of fancy terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Fred's no-nonsense attitude and class consciousness.

jokes.text: No, you don't.
jokes.setup: Will claiming he knows the feeling of earning money
jokes.punchline: Fred's flat contradiction
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery that cuts through Will's pretension with brutal honesty.

jokes.text: I think you're a bourgie little toad.
jokes.setup: Fred being honest about his opinion of Will
jokes.punchline: Blunt, colorful insult
jokes.explanation: Memorable insult with great word choice ('bourgie little toad') that perfectly captures Fred's working-class perspective.

jokes.text: Out of girlfriend for a long time.
jokes.setup: Will about to brag about his romantic experience to girlfriend's father
jokes.punchline: Will catches himself mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment showing Will's social awareness kicking in at the last second.

jokes.text: I'm telling you, he's a low-class working stiff like yourself. Even lower.
jokes.setup: Will claiming the real him is working class like Fred
jokes.punchline: Will's enthusiastic over-correction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke where Will's eagerness to connect leads to self-deprecation.

jokes.text: Those are Twinkies.
jokes.setup: Hilary excited about finding gold bars in safe
jokes.punchline: Carlton's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Great visual misdirection with perfect deadpan delivery from Carlton.

jokes.text: I meant me.
jokes.setup: Carlton saying someone's coming into money
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey clarifying he means himself (through blackmail)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey moment - opportunistic and sarcastic with excellent economy.

jokes.text: A week.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey asking for $1000 to stay quiet
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's $1000 per week
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with Geoffrey's characteristic opportunism and timing.

jokes.text: Last thing I ever wanna do again is see a naked middle-aged man in the shower.
jokes.setup: Will complaining about Fred following him everywhere
jokes.punchline: Specific uncomfortable detail about gym shower
jokes.explanation: Good specificity creates genuine cringe humor about overly attached father figure.

jokes.text: You've never been there, have you?
jokes.setup: Lisa asking if Fred will miss Cleveland
jokes.punchline: Fred's matter-of-fact response about Cleveland's undesirability
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery about Cleveland with great economy and timing.

jokes.text: You're just too damn lovable.
jokes.setup: Lisa blaming Will for the situation
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic 'character flaw' of being too likeable
jokes.explanation: Good ironic take on Will's charm becoming a problem.

jokes.text: Uh, Daddy, remember Craig?
jokes.setup: Fred says Will is like the son he never had
jokes.punchline: Lisa awkwardly reminds him he actually has a son named Craig
jokes.explanation: Perfectly timed awkward reminder. Lisa's diplomatic correction highlights Fred's selective memory about his actual son.

jokes.text: What kind of idiot picks a password no one can guess?
jokes.setup: Struggling to guess Phil's computer password
jokes.punchline: Hilary's backwards logic about password security
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary logic - completely backwards reasoning delivered with total confidence.

jokes.text: Never should've let you play with those Menendez boys.
jokes.setup: Kids looking through his will
jokes.punchline: Phil's reference to infamous murder case
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy reference to real case with perfect timing and delivery.

jokes.text: Nice earrings.
jokes.setup: Phil giving heartfelt praise to each child
jokes.punchline: Phil's shallow compliment to Hilary
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast joke - meaningful praise for others, surface-level comment for Hilary. Great structure.

jokes.text: Probably say, 'Hey, Fred, you got change for a 20?'
jokes.setup: Fred worried about pastor seeing him at strip club
jokes.punchline: Will's implication that pastor frequents the place
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of Fred's moral worry with Will's street-smart cynicism.

jokes.text: Two, three times a day.
jokes.setup: Fred asking how often Will comes to strip club
jokes.punchline: Will's exaggerated frequency
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation joke with Will's typical exaggeration for effect.

jokes.text: except I work with my hands and they work with everything else.
jokes.setup: Fred comparing his honest work to strippers
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic description of stripping
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that maintains Fred's character while being risqué for network TV.

jokes.text: Did it ever occur to you I might wanna see this game more than I wanna see you?
jokes.setup: Will trying to get Fred to take Lisa on date instead
jokes.punchline: Will's deliberately insulting question to Lisa
jokes.explanation: Good cringe setup for Will's desperate plan to alienate Fred.

jokes.text: Don't you see how important this game is to Will and I?
jokes.setup: Fred expected to defend Lisa against Will's rudeness
jokes.punchline: Fred sides with Will against his own daughter
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection showing how Will's plan completely backfires - Fred chooses him over Lisa.

jokes.text: I gotta break up with your father.
jokes.setup: Will's relationship problems with Fred
jokes.punchline: Will treating it like romantic breakup
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist concept treating father-figure relationship like romantic one.

jokes.text: There's someone else.
jokes.setup: Will's 'breakup' with Fred continues
jokes.punchline: Using classic breakup line about Uncle Phil
jokes.explanation: Perfect continuation of romantic breakup metaphor with classic 'someone else' revelation.

jokes.text: Oh, who is he?
jokes.setup: Will saying there's someone else
jokes.punchline: Fred playing along with romantic breakup scenario
jokes.explanation: Good commitment to the absurd premise by having Fred accept the romantic framing.

jokes.text: Can I call you?
jokes.setup: Breakup scene continuing
jokes.punchline: Fred asking classic post-breakup question
jokes.explanation: Continues romantic breakup metaphor with typical desperate ex-boyfriend behavior.

jokes.text: Same game, next season.
jokes.setup: Fred's message to Will through Lisa
jokes.punchline: Sports metaphor for their friendship continuing
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to episode title with sports metaphor that captures their renewed friendship.

jokes.text: No, don't need to. You already came true.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggesting Will make a wish on shooting star
jokes.punchline: Will's romantic line about Lisa being his wish
jokes.explanation: Sweet but predictable romantic ending line that fits Will's charm.
